human_prompt: Please provide definitions for the missing matrix elements and semantic conventions listed below. The automated derivation is blocked until these are resolved.

missing_entities.description: The definition of the matrix element H_{ij} = <i|H|j> is not found in any source file or derivation note. The Hamiltonian operator H acting on the basis states |i>, |j> must be explicitly given.
missing_entities.impact: Blocks all simplifications that depend on the explicit form of H_{ij}
missing_entities.suggested_resolution: Supply the explicit formula for H_{ij} in terms of the basis states, e.g., H_{ij} = E_i delta_{ij} + V_{ij}

missing_entities.description: It is unclear whether <i| is the left eigenvector (bra) of the unperturbed or perturbed Hamiltonian. The orientation convention for the dual basis must be specified.
missing_entities.impact: Blocks correct index placement in tensor contractions involving bras and kets
missing_entities.suggested_resolution: Declare whether <i| denotes the unperturbed left eigenstate, the biorthogonal dual, or the Hermitian conjugate of |i>

missing_entities.description: The roles of external indices a, b, c are not assigned: which are incoming/outgoing, which label band states vs. momentum states, and whether they run over the full Hilbert space or a restricted subspace.
missing_entities.impact: Blocks correct classification of free and dummy indices in tensor contractions
missing_entities.suggested_resolution: Label each external index with its physical role: band index, momentum, spin, etc., and specify its range.

missing_entities.description: The sign and factorial conventions for Taylor expansion terms (e.g., 1/n! prefactors) are not specified. Different conventions (physicist vs. mathematician) yield different signs in the effective Hamiltonian.
missing_entities.impact: Blocks verification that the derived sigma coefficients match the reference
missing_entities.suggested_resolution: State the Taylor expansion convention: e.g., H_eff = H + [H,V]/omega + ... with explicit prefactor rules

missing_entities.description: The sign of the source operator S in the generating functional Z[J] = ∫ Dphi exp(iS[phi] + i∫ J phi) is not fixed. The sign convention affects the sign of all n-point correlators.
missing_entities.impact: Blocks sign consistency across derived expressions
missing_entities.suggested_resolution: Specify the sign convention for the source coupling term

missing_entities.description: The claimed identities reference a source document that could not be located. Without the authoritative source, the claims cannot be independently verified.
missing_entities.impact: Blocks validation that claimed identities are correctly transcribed
missing_entities.suggested_resolution: Provide the exact citation (DOI/arXiv ID + equation numbers) for each claimed identity

allowed_work.description: Linting and formatting of existing code
allowed_work.rationale: Does not depend on missing semantics

forbidden_work.rationale: Missing H_{ij} definition means factorization may be incorrect
Missing index role assignment means contractions may be invalid
Missing source authority means validation target is unknown
